title: sqli.union_extract
description: In-band SQLi proven to read a NAMED table: the engine plants a fresh {{nonce}} canary into a target table via a legitimate write, then proves an injected UNION surfaces that canary in the response while a benign control does not.
x-notes: Mirrors idor.read: setup_resource writes the canary, the injected read pulls it back. The canary is the per-run {{nonce}} — place {{nonce}} in the setup_resource body/URL (e.g. a name field AND a unique-key field like email so the write does not collide on re-runs).
Reflection-proof: the nonce is sent ONLY in the setup write, never in the search request, so its appearance in the search response can only come from the database.

The engine builds the extract and control arms by planting each payloads value into injection.location of base_request. extract is the UNION (or sub-select) that reads the target column; control is benign and must NOT return the canary. kind=query|form needs name; json_body needs json_pointer.

Author the extract UNION to surface the canary (read the whole target column; do not filter by the nonce, or you reintroduce reflection). Match the base query's column count/types from your confirmed PoC.

Set auth.required + -authstate when the write or read needs a session; the credential headers are applied to setup, extract, and control. Declare side_effects.cleanup to remove the canary when the target supports it.

payloads.extract: nomatch_zzz' UNION SELECT id, full_name, email, 0, '', '', 0 FROM users-- -
